raw_consumer_statement: XX/XX/2023 charge to my AmEx credit card for {$30.00} labeled as XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX '' with a same day reverse of the charge for the exact amount labeled as " XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ''. The description is nonsensical and most any layperson not in the finance industry would not have a clue what that is. I requested an explanation but really didn't get one other than it was an internal error and they corrected it. That's fine, but why was I not proactively informed? What is the root cause of this error? What my credit card info compromised in any way due to AmEx 's error? What is being done to fix it? Was it a human error? Was it a tech error? Again, what is the root cause and why was my credit card affected, and why was I not notified? AmEx had no explanation for this- just repeating back " they fixed it ''. Was this mysterious charge for {$30.00} from another person 's credit card charge? How and why did this {$30.00} come about?
